text: Here, the world feels both still and expectant. The glass meeting room, immaculate and silent, speaks of order and anticipation—a space awaiting ideas or collaboration. Then, in the vibrant, text-covered exhibit, I sense playful chaos and curiosity. The words on the wall suggest confusion isn’t just real but necessary—a rhythm to embrace. I feel like a participant-observer, balancing between methodical precision and poetic uncertainty, comforted by the thought that meaning sometimes arrives long after the moment has passed.
